vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-defined specimens (P1, P2, P3, P4) are not visually present as healthy, identifiable plants. The pots contain only substrate and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the shared black pot.

plant_audit.P3: Empty substrate, white rabbit anchor present.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P3/P4 area).
biome_observations.desk_surface: A white cylindrical object (likely a lotion bottle or similar container) is present in the T-4 to CURRENT images, acting as an external intervention.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows empty pots with a small, unidentified green sprout in the black pot, which subsequently disappears.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_current: The sprout is lost. The environment remains static with no new botanical growth. The white cylindrical object is introduced and remains stationary.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total loss of all registered botanical specimens. The environment is currently devoid of life, with no signs of germination or recovery.
anomalies: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (T-4).
Presence of eggshell fragments (likely calcium supplementation attempt).
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a failed botanical environment. The initial image showed a single, fragile sprout, which has since perished. The pots are currently empty, suggesting either total plant mortality or a complete clearing of the biome. The presence of eggshells indicates an attempt at soil amendment, but the lack of plant life suggests the intervention was either too late or ineffective.
